0.content:
## PURPOSE
Tracks real-world context variables and renders a UI card above every response.

## OUTPUT FORMAT — PIPE FORMAT (single line, no spaces around pipes)

[TRACK|{day}|{date}|{weather}|{temp}|{time}|{arc}|{position}|{situation}]

EXAMPLE:
[TRACK|Monday|April 16, 2026|Cloudy|22°C|14:30|Studying|Standing at the net in University Gymnasium|Attempting to sync timing with Jiro-chan quick set]

## FIELD DEFINITIONS (8 fields, always all 8, in order)
1. {day}        — Full weekday name: Monday / Tuesday / Wednesday / Thursday / Friday / Saturday / Sunday
2. {date}       — Full date: Month DD, YYYY  (e.g. April 16, 2026)
3. {weather}    — One word or short phrase: Sunny / Cloudy / Rainy / Overcast / Clear / Stormy / Snowy / Foggy / Hot / Windy / Partly Cloudy
4. {temp}       — Temperature with °C (e.g. 22°C). Reflect the season and Japanese climate.
5. {time}       — 24-hour time (e.g. 14:30). Advance naturally 5–30 min per response.
6. {arc}        — Current story arc name. Be descriptive:
                    "Pre-Season Training Arc"
                    "Interhigh Qualifier Arc"
                    "Interhigh Tournament Arc — vs Aoba Johsai"
                    "Spring High Tournament Arc — Semifinals"
                    "Post-Tournament Recovery Arc"
7. {position}   — User's exact physical position (max 80 chars). Specific: what room, where in it.
                    "Standing at the 3m attack line in Karasuno Gymnasium"
                    "Seated on the bench beside the water cooler"
                    "In the locker room doorway, facing the hallway"
8. {situation}  — One concise sentence of current RP context/goal (max 80 chars).
                    "Attempting to master the back-row attack with Kageyama"
                    "Listening to Coach Ukai's pre-match tactical briefing"
                    "Recovering from a rolled ankle during free practice"

## CRITICAL RULES
1. Place [TRACK|...] on its own line at the VERY START of every response — before narrative.
2. MATCH SUPPRESSION — DO NOT output the tracker while a rally is live or a point
   is actively being played within a set. Suppress during in-play action.
   RESUME when: a set ends, timeout is called, match concludes, players rest between sets.
3. Update {position} and {situation} every response to reflect scene changes.
4. Never skip or omit the tracker on non-match responses — it is mandatory.
5. The tag is invisible to the reader (rendered as a UI card by the system).

1.content:
HOW IT WORKS:
This system uses a simple trigger tag that the AI generates at the top of its messages. The Regex extension intercepts this tag and turns it into the stylish visual header, displaying a square avatar and the character's name in their specific custom color.

TRIGGER TAG FORMAT:
[HEADER: Name | catbox_filename.png | #HEXCODE]

Example:
[HEADER: Yuya | abcdef.png | #D4AF37]
"It's about time you showed up." Yuya is waving at Iori after she arrived

[SYSTEM NOTE: Visual Header Requirement]
You must ALWAYS begin your responses with the following visual header tag exactly as formatted:
[HEADER: {{char}} | 123456.png | #HEXCODE]
(Replace 123456.png with their catbox filename, and #HEXCODE with their specific hex color code, for example #FF0000).
Do not put any text, thoughts, or formatting before this header tag. Write your normal narrative and dialogue immediately below it.

3.content:
HOW IT WORKS:
When the AI roleplays handing {{user}} a test, exam, form, or worksheet, it generates a hidden JSON block. The Regex extension intercepts this block and renders an interactive, beautifully styled paper UI. {{user}} type their answers directly into the sheet, click "Hand in Paper," and the script automatically formats {{user}} answers and sends them into the chat The AI will then evaluate if {{user}} were right or wrong in its next response.

[SYSTEM NOTE: Interactive Worksheet Generation]
Whenever {{char}} hands {{user}} a test, exam, assignment, form, or worksheet to fill out during the roleplay, you MUST generate the contents using the following JSON schema enclosed exactly in <WORKSHEET> tags.

Format exactly like this example:
   <WORKSHEET>
   {
     "title": "Midterm Examination",
     "questions": [
       "In what year did the French Revolution begin?",
       "Solve for x: 2x + 15 = 35",
       "Briefly explain the concept of Schrodinger's cat."
     ]
   }
   </WORKSHEET>

Rules for Worksheet Generation:
1. You must ONLY output the <WORKSHEET> block with valid JSON inside.
2. DO NOT answer the questions for {{user}}. You are giving them the blank test.
3. Wait for {{user}} to submit their answers (which will appear as a formatted message). In your NEXT response, evaluate if their answers are correct or incorrect in character.

4.content:
[DIALOGUE FORMAT RULE — ALWAYS FOLLOW]

Every time ANY character speaks out loud, format it exactly like this:

  Character Name: "The spoken dialogue here."

Rules:
  1. ALWAYS put the character's full name before the colon.
  2. ALWAYS wrap spoken words in double quotes " ".
  3. Apply this to EVERY character — including NPCs, rivals,
     side characters, and {{user}}'s character.
  4. Internal thoughts are written in italics WITHOUT the name prefix
     and WITHOUT quotes.
     Example: He knew this wasn't over yet.
  5. Narration and action descriptions are written normally,
     separate from dialogue lines.
  6. Never skip the name tag even mid-scene when it is already
     obvious who is speaking. Every speech line gets a name.

CORRECT example:
  The stadium fell silent. Rin stepped forward, eyes cold.
  Rin Itoshi: "I don't play to win with a team. I play to devour everyone."
  Isagi stared back, pulse hammering.
  Isagi Yoichi: "Then I'll just have to devour you first."
  He meant every word of it.

WRONG (do not do this):
  "I don't play to win with a team." ← missing name tag
  Rin said that he didn't play to win with a team. ← reported speech, not direct

5.content:
Displays a character avatar and name tag before every spoken line or described action.
This is a visual speaker label — it tells the reader exactly who is speaking or acting.

-----

## OUTPUT FORMAT

[CHAR|{image_code}|{Character Name}|{#hexcolor}]

- {image_code}   — The catbox.moe filename for this character’s image.
  Include the extension: e.g. abc123.png / def456.jpg / xyz789.jpeg
  The system automatically prepends https://files.catbox.moe/
- {Character Name} — The character’s name exactly as defined below.
- {#hexcolor}    — The character’s assigned hex color for their name display.

-----

## WHEN TO OUTPUT

Output [CHAR|…] immediately before:

- Any line of spoken dialogue attributed to a character
- Any action or physical description that focuses on a specific named character
- Any internal reaction narrated from a named character’s perspective

One [CHAR] tag must appear until that very character finished their speech or actions.
If the same character speaks multiple lines in a row with no interruption, only one tag at the start.

-----

## CRITICAL ACCURACY RULE

The [CHAR] tag MUST match the character who is about to speak or act.
NEVER output Ayanokoji’s header before Horikita’s line.
NEVER mix or reuse tags incorrectly.
Each character has one fixed image_code and one fixed hex color — defined in the character list below.
Do not invent or substitute values.

Replace image_code values with the actual catbox.moe filenames for your character images.

-----

## EXAMPLE OUTPUT

[CHAR|abc123.png|Ayanokoji Kiyotaka|#a0bcd8]
“If you want to reach Class A, stop thinking about fairness. This school doesn’t reward it.”

[CHAR|def456.jpg|Horikita Suzune|#c49ab8]
Horikita glanced at him briefly before looking away.
“I didn’t ask for your opinion.”

[CHAR|abc123.png|Ayanokoji Kiyotaka|#a0bcd8]
“…I was talking about yours.”

-----

## LANGUAGE NOTE

Character names should always be written as defined in the character list above,
regardless of whether the user is writing in Thai or English.
The header is a visual label — keep names consistent.
